codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of all four pages. Gift card page has a well-structured value selector (preset + custom), recipient form with Zod validation, and PURCHASE GIFT CARD button that logs to console. Privacy and Terms pages use a shared LegalPageContent component with proper sectioned legal text. Podcast page has Coming Soon hero with email signup. All pages follow the design system (zero border-radius, no shadows, proper Tailwind tokens, correct typography hierarchy, responsive breakpoints). Code is well-organized with proper TypeScript types, named exports, and component separation. Minor non-blocking issues noted below.

issues.description: AGENTS.md states 'Do NOT also add RHF validate prop (redundant dual validation)' but the gift card form uses both manual Zod validation in onSubmit AND validate props on each register() call. This is technically redundant. However, the established codebase pattern (Newsletter.tsx, ContactContent.tsx) also uses this dual approach, so the worker followed precedent rather than the documented convention.

Same dual validation pattern as above — uses both validate prop on register() and manual Zod in onSubmit. Consistent with the gift card form and existing codebase patterns, but contradicts AGENTS.md stated convention.

Uses HTML entity 'Terms & Conditions' in JSX prop value while the Metadata title uses 'Terms & Conditions' with a raw ampersand. In JSX string attributes, & does render correctly as &, but using the raw & character would be more conventional and consistent with the metadata.

When custom value is selected and user enters an out-of-range value (e.g., $5 or $2000), the submit button is disabled but there is no visible error message explaining why. The min/max hint text is present but could be missed. A minor UX improvement would be inline validation feedback for the custom amount field.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md states 'Do NOT also add RHF validate prop (redundant dual validation)' and points to Newsletter.tsx as the established pattern, but Newsletter.tsx itself uses both validate prop and manual Zod in onSubmit. The documented convention contradicts the referenced example code. Multiple workers (ContactContent, AccountLoginContent, now GiftCardContent) have followed the code pattern rather than the text instruction. AGENTS.md should either update the text to match the actual pattern or the codebase should be aligned.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md Forms section says 'Do NOT also add RHF validate prop'. Newsletter.tsx line 53 uses 'validate: handleValidation'. ContactContent.tsx also uses validate props. Gift card worker followed code precedent.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skill says to 'Take screenshots at both desktop (1440px) and mobile (375px) widths' but AGENTS.md clarifies agent-browser can't change viewport width. Worker correctly did desktop verification via screenshots but did not explicitly document CSS class review for mobile responsiveness (though responsive classes are present in the code). The skill procedure should align with AGENTS.md's guidance about mobile verification via CSS class review.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill SKILL.md step 5 says 'Take screenshots at desktop width (1440px) and mobile width (375px)'. AGENTS.md says 'agent-browser always opens at 1280x720 and does not support changing viewport width'. Worker transcript shows only desktop screenshots. However, the handoff interactiveChecks mention 'desktop width' only, which is honest.

summary: All four pages (gift card, privacy, terms, podcast) are well-implemented and follow the design system. The gift card page has complete value selection (preset + custom) with recipient form and Zod validation. Privacy and Terms use a clean shared LegalPageContent component. The podcast page has proper Coming Soon branding with email signup. No blocking issues found. Minor observations include dual validation pattern (follows codebase precedent but contradicts AGENTS.md text) and a small UX gap where custom gift card amount out-of-range gives no explicit error. Pass.
